findings.title: Matched-entry regression test no longer scopes the resolved item
findings.description: This test used to assert against the specific log entry containing the unique gibberish token. It now picks any pending entry and only checks aggregate dot counts, so a different pending item elsewhere on the page could satisfy the assertion and hide a regression in the matched entry itself.
findings.fix: Keep the assertion scoped to the unique logged entry, or additionally verify that the entry containing the gibberish token changed from 1 pending dot to 1 resolved dot.

findings.title: Water-modal color check is asserting the wrong SVG arc
findings.description: The test claims to verify the sky-blue water accent, but `svg circle`.nth(1) is the primary total-fluids arc. In `CircularProgressRing`, the water segment is rendered as the final secondary arc, so this assertion would still pass if the water color regressed.
findings.fix: Assert the secondary arc color instead of the primary arc, ideally by targeting the water segment explicitly rather than relying on raw circle order.

findings.title: New truncation contract is only covered on the happy path
findings.description: The refactor added row caps and `isTruncated` warnings in `convex/aggregateQueries.ts` and `convex/foodAssessments.ts`, but the updated tests only assert `isTruncated === false` on tiny fixtures. There is no regression test that forces the cap and proves the new contract behaves correctly when the result set is truncated.
findings.fix: Add a fixture that exceeds the cap and assert `isTruncated === true` plus the bounded result size for both `allFoodTrials` and `allFoods`.
